host_model_constraints: The selected Envoy HTTP ext_authz service observes a pre-upstream authorization request and cannot inspect the later upstream response headers or body.
The checked-in ext_authz configuration buffers at most 4096 request-body bytes with partial messages disabled; the existing minimal smoke sends no request body.
Only the headers allowed by the ext_authz authorization_request configuration are forwarded to the authorization service.
The authorization service currently sees the Envoy-to-service socket endpoints and does not map original downstream connection endpoints into Common connection metadata.

connection_metadata.reason: The HTTP authorization service maps its local Envoy peer socket rather than the original downstream connection metadata.

request_headers.reason: The ext_authz HTTP request forwards an allowlisted header set into the Common Runtime mapper; canonical header coverage is pending.

request_body_buffered.reason: Envoy with_request_body and the connector buffered body mode are both configured at 4096 bytes, but the existing host smoke sends no body.

request_body_streaming.reason: The selected ext_authz integration deliberately uses the with_request_body buffered authorization request, not a streaming body protocol.

response_headers.reason: HTTP ext_authz runs before the upstream request and does not receive the later upstream response headers.

response_body_buffered.reason: HTTP ext_authz does not receive the later upstream response body.

response_body_streaming.reason: HTTP ext_authz does not receive an upstream response body stream.

phase1.reason: The authorization service maps request headers into the Common Runtime Phase-1 path; a canonical baseline result is pending.

phase2.reason: Buffered request-body forwarding and Common Runtime Phase 2 are configured, but no existing host smoke proves that Envoy delivered the body.

phase3.reason: The pre-upstream ext_authz call cannot inspect upstream response headers.

phase4.reason: The pre-upstream ext_authz call cannot inspect upstream response bodies.

phase4_rule_evaluation.reason: The selected Envoy ext_authz integration executes before the upstream response and does not expose upstream response-body data for Phase-4 rule evaluation.

phase4_pre_commit_deny.reason: Because ext_authz never sees the later upstream response, it has no Phase-4 response point at which to deny before the response commits.

late_intervention.reason: The authorization decision completes before the upstream response starts, so there is no response-phase late intervention point.

late_intervention_log_only.reason: The selected pre-upstream ext_authz host model has no committed upstream response on which to apply a late log-only intervention.

late_intervention_abort.reason: The selected pre-upstream ext_authz host model has no upstream response-phase point from which the authorization service can perform a late connection abort.

late_intervention_status_metadata.reason: The selected ext_authz integration does not observe an upstream response, so it cannot produce Phase-4 metadata that distinguishes original and visible upstream-response status or late actions.

deny.reason: A disruptive Common Runtime decision is returned as a non-2xx ext_authz response; a fresh canonical deny result is pending.

redirect.reason: The Common Runtime preserves redirect status and URL, but the repository HTTP authorization response does not emit the Location header.

drop.reason: The HTTP authorization contract returns an authorization response and cannot directly silent-drop the downstream connection.

abort_connection.reason: The external authorization service cannot directly abort Envoy's downstream connection as a ModSecurity action.

log_only.reason: A non-disruptive rule leaves the authorization response successful; explicit canonical log-only evidence is pending.

transaction_id.reason: The configured x-request-id header is resolved by Common Runtime and included in decision events; canonical correlation evidence is pending.

event_jsonl.reason: Common Runtime writes bounded metadata-only disruptive-decision JSONL; canonical field and payload-absence evidence is pending.

config_inline_rules.reason: The repository HTTP authorization service configuration parser and Common Runtime support inline rules; canonical config evidence is pending.

config_rules_file.reason: The checked-in service path loads a local rules file through Common Runtime; canonical config evidence is pending.

config_remote_rules.reason: Common Runtime exposes remote rule key and URL configuration, but the no-CRS baseline does not exercise external networking.

transport_metadata.reason: The pre-upstream ext_authz service does not observe client-visible upstream-response commitment or transport outcomes.

request_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The selected ext_authz integration uses a bounded buffered authorization body, not incremental request-body ingestion.

response_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The pre-upstream ext_authz service never receives an upstream response-body stream.

phase4_end_of_stream_evaluation.reason: HTTP ext_authz never receives the later upstream response stream or its end-of-stream event.

content_type_scope.reason: The selected ext_authz path does not receive an upstream response Content-Type or response body to scope.

header_limits.reason: The ext_authz header allowlist is not a connector-local canonical header-limit enforcement contract.

request_body_limits.reason: Envoy with_request_body is configured with a 4096-byte bound, but no canonical host limit case has exercised it.

response_body_limits.reason: HTTP ext_authz does not receive an upstream response body on which to enforce a response-body limit.

no_full_response_buffering.reason: The request-only ext_authz host model has no upstream response stream for this property to describe.

first_byte_before_response_end.reason: The request-only ext_authz service cannot observe an Envoy client first byte or upstream response end.

http1_content_length.reason: Envoy can proxy HTTP/1.1 responses, but no canonical ext_authz transport case covers Content-Length handling.

http1_chunked.reason: Envoy can proxy chunked HTTP/1.1 responses, but no canonical ext_authz transport case covers them.

keep_alive.reason: No canonical sequential-request keep-alive run is attached to the Envoy ext_authz path.

parallel_requests.reason: The Envoy ext_authz full-lifecycle catalog has no parallel-request evidence path.

http2.reason: Envoy HTTP/2 transport behavior is not exercised by a canonical ext_authz full-lifecycle case.

client_abort.reason: The external authorization service cannot observe or directly control an Envoy downstream client-abort lifecycle.

upstream_abort.reason: The pre-upstream authorization service cannot observe or control an upstream response-abort lifecycle.

response_body_decompression.reason: HTTP ext_authz does not receive an upstream response body to decompress.

source_contract.reason: Service, mapper, metadata, Envoy configuration, and harness sources are present; no canonical source-contract result is attached.

compile.reason: A repository authorization-service build path exists; no current canonical compile result is attached.

link.reason: The service links Common Runtime and libmodsecurity; no current canonical link result is attached.

config_load.reason: Connector config check and real Envoy validation paths exist; no current canonical result is attached.

start_smoke.reason: Envoy and authorization-service start wiring exists; no current canonical request-free result is attached.

minimal_runtime_smoke.reason: A real Envoy ext_authz 200/403 harness exists; no current canonical minimal-runtime result is attached.

no_crs_baseline.reason: Capability-selected pre-upstream cases can run through the host path, but no canonical baseline result is attached.

crs_smoke.reason: Legacy limited CRS smoke wiring exists and is not baseline evidence.

extended_matrix.reason: Extended-matrix verification is outside this canonical no-CRS declaration.
